}} '''Gareth Hock''' (born '''Gareth Charnock''', 5 September 1983) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr|lf}} or {{rlp|pr}}. He last played for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Hock began his career at [[Wigan Warriors]] in 2003, and had spells playing for [[Widnes Vikings]], [[Salford Red Devils]], [[Leigh Centurions]], [[Featherstone Rovers]] and [[Barrow Raiders]]. He returned to Leigh Centurions before retiring in 2019. He won 4 caps for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and 5 for [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level. ==Early life== Wigan-born Gareth Charnock joined [[Wigan Warriors]]' scholarship scheme from St Jude's, a local amateur team. He changed his surname after his parents separated.{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2003/apr/13/rugbyleague.wiganwarriors|title=Hock is Wigan's Rooney|newspaper=The Observer|date=13 April 2003|access-date=15 August 2013}} ==Career== ===2000s=== Hock gained international experience playing in the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] Academy side that toured Australia in 2001. He worked his way up through the Wigan Warriors' scholarship and Academy systems, playing in the Academy Under-19s team during the 2002 season, but injury forced him to miss much of the latter part of the year. He was also called up to the [[England national rugby league team|England]] Academy squad to face the Aussie Schoolboys in December 2002. He played in the first Test, scoring a vital try in England's historic victory. He had achieved amateur representative honours, touring with Great Britain U16s and U18s. [[File:Gareth Hock England.jpg|thumb|150px|right|Hock lining up for England at the 2008 RLWC]] He was included in the Wigan Warriors' 2003 first team squad by head coach [[Stuart Raper]]. Hock made his first team début that season against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in the [[Challenge Cup]], scoring two tries after coming on as a substitute. He went on to become a first team regular for the rest of 2003's [[Super League VIII]]. He was included in the England A squad to face the Australians at [[Griffin Park]], [[Brentford]] on 28 October 2003. He was named Super League Young Player of the Year. Hock played for Wigan from the interchange bench in the [[2003 Super League Grand Final]] which was lost to [[Bradford Bulls]]. Hock signed a new one-year contract with the Wigan Warriors in June 2004. He picked up a [[medial cruciate ligament]] (MCL) knee injury against the [[Bradford Bulls]] on 25 February 2005. The injury required a full knee reconstruction which kept him sidelined until the end of the 2005 [[Super League X]]. He was expected to make his return in 2006's [[Super League XI]] but suffered a stress fracture to his shin and had to miss the start of 2006's [[Super League XI]]. On 19 May 2006 Hock made his return in the Wigan Warriors' [[Challenge Cup]] match against the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] which Wigan lost 4–16; despite being on the losing team Hock made an impressive return.{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2006/may/20/rugbyleague.sport|title=Rutgerson helps power Salford past careworn Warriors|first=Andy|last=Wilson|newspaper=The Observer|date=20 May 2006|access-date=15 August 2013}} He featured in the next match which was against rivals [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], he was again impressive and scored a try but the Warriors lost 14–28. In September 2008, he was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]],{{cite web|url=http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|title=Myler gets England call|publisher=England Rugby League|date=29 September 2008|access-date=2008-10-03|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081007232803/http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|archive-date=7 October 2008 }} and in October 2008 he was named in the final 24-man [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England]] squad.{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7656553.stm|title=Purdham earns World Cup call-up|publisher=BBC|date=7 October 2008|access-date=2008-10-07}} He was named in the [[England national rugby league team|England]] team to face [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at the [[Keepmoat Stadium]], [[Doncaster]], before England's departure for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7642490.stm|title=Gleeson to lead new-look England|publisher=BBC|date=9 October 2008|access-date=2008-10-10}} He went with the [[England national rugby league team|England squad]] to compete in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] tournament in Australia. [[2008 Rugby League World Cup Group A|Group A]]'s first match against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] he played at {{rlp|sr}} in England's victory. In June 2009, he tested positive for [[cocaine]] following tests on an 'A' sample of [[urine]] taken during a match between the Wigan Warriors and the Salford City Reds.{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=14464|title=RFL Statement on Hock|publisher=superleague.com|date=22 June 2009|access-date=2009-06-22|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120405033129/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=14464|archive-date=5 April 2012 }}{{cite web|url=http://www.wiganwarriors.com/news_full.asp?newsid=4880|title=Wigan Warriors official statement|publisher=Wigan Warriors|date=23 June 2009|access-date=2009-06-23|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090626003929/http://www.wiganwarriors.com/news_full.asp?newsid=4880|archive-date=2009-06-26}} A second 'B' sample also returned a positive result for [[benzoylecgonine]], the main metabolite of cocaine.{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=14605|title=Wigan Warriors official statement|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=7 July 2009|access-date=2009-07-07|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120819212607/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=14605|archive-date=19 August 2012 }} In August 2009, it was confirmed that Hock would face a two-year ban from rugby league as he did not appeal against the mandatory ban.{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=15170|title=Hock two-year ban confirmed|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=26 August 2009|access-date=2009-08-26|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120825214758/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=15170|archive-date=25 August 2012 }} ===2010s=== On 10 May 2011, Gareth Hock signed a new contract with Wigan until 2015, beginning once his 2-year doping ban has ended on 23 June.{{cite web |url=http://www.wiganwarriors.com/WContent.aspx?id=5969&type=1 |title=Hock Signs 5 Year Deal |access-date=2011-05-11 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120325093105/http://www.wiganwarriors.com/WContent.aspx?id=5969&type=1 |archive-date=25 March 2012 }} He made a high-profile return against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in a 2011 Super League game. Since his return from his ban, he was a consistent member of the Wigan first team, gaining the number 12 jersey in the 2012 season (following the departure of [[Joel Tomkins]] to [[Saracens F.C.|Saracens RUFC]]). Hock is well known for his aggressive and confrontational style of play and has begun to return to the high standard performances seen before his ban. On 22 January 2013, Wigan announced that Hock would be joining [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] on a season-long loan.{{cite web|url=http://www.wiganwarriors.com/news/2013-01-22-hock-joins-widnes-vikings-on-season-loan|title=Hock Joins Widnes Vikings on Season Loan|publisher=Wigan Warriors|date=22 January 2013|access-date=21 January 2018}} After a possible move to NRL side [[Parramatta Eels]] fell through,{{Cite web | url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/parramatta-eels-sign-english-international-forwards-gareth-hock-and-lee-mossop/story-e6frexq0-1226628242010 | title=Stuart signs English pair| date=23 April 2013}} on 5 September 2013 [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] announced he would be signing for them for the 2014 Super League season on a 4-year deal.{{cite web | url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/28788/salford-announce-raft-of-new | title=Super League }} On 22 October 2013, England Rugby League issued the following statement: "As a result of serious breaches of team discipline that have fallen below the strict code of conduct as agreed by team management, Gareth Hock has been withdrawn from the England [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|Rugby League World Cup]] squad with immediate effect. He is replaced by Huddersfield player Brett Ferres. There will be no further comment issued by England Rugby League on this matter."{{cite web|url = https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24641559|title= Gareth Hock was 'unfairly treated'|date=23 October 2013|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=23 October 2013}} In 2015 Gareth moved to play for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in the Kingstone Press Championship.{{Cite web|title = Gareth Hock turns back on Super League offers to join Leigh|url = https://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12040/9733377/gareth-hock-turns-back-on-super-league-offers-to-join-leigh|access-date = 2015-04-29}} He made his début in a comfortable 54–6 victory over [[Workington Town]].{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleaguechampionships.co.uk/match_day/match_report/11041|title=Match report|website=rugbyleaguechampionships.co.uk|access-date=23 May 2023|archive-date=19 January 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180119120840/http://www.rugbyleaguechampionships.co.uk/match_day/match_report/11041|url-status=dead}} In January 2018 he joined [[Featherstone Rovers]] on a one-year deal.{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11207528/featherstone-sign-gareth-hock-on-one-year-deal|title=Featherstone sign Gareth Hock on one-year deal|publisher=Sky Sports|date=14 January 2018|access-date=19 January 2018}} In August 2018 Featherstone Rovers terminated his contract. On 23 October 2018 he joined Barrow Raiders. In May 2019, following his release from Barrow, Hock re-joined Leigh Centurions. In July 2019, he announced his retirement from rugby league with immediate effect.{{cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/former-wigan-warriors-star-gareth-hock-makes-scary-decision-to-retire-from-rugby-league-1-9881487|title=Former Wigan Warriors star Gareth Hock makes "scary" decision to retire from rugby league|date=17 July 2019 }} ==Personal life== In March 2023, Hock appeared at Bolton Crown court after being charged with ten offences including assault by beating, coercive, controlling behaviour and perverting the course of justice.{{cite web |title=Ex-Wigan Warriors and England rugby league international to stand trial accused of rape |url=https://www.itv.com/news/granada/2023-03-22/former-england-rugby-league-player-to-stand-trial-accused-of-rape |website=ITV |access-date=28 March 2025 |date=22 March 2023}} A ten-day trial was set by Judge Martin Walsh for April 2024 where Hock will be questioned by prosecutors, with a review hearing set for May. Hock was bailed under specific conditions that included surrendering his passport, wearing a GPS tracking tag and sleeping every night at his home address.{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/gareth-hock-court-rape-charge-29522100/|title=Ex-GB and England rugby league player Gareth Hock appears in court after rape charge|website=Daily Mirror}} ==References== {{Reflist}} == External links == *[https://www.featherstonerovers.co.uk/squad/40 Featherstone Rovers profile]{{Dead link|date=July 2025 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }} *[http://leighrl.co.uk/wp/team_member/12-gareth-hock/ Leigh Centurions profile] *[http://wigan.rlfans.com/readarticle.php?article_id=530 Gareth Hock Statistics at wigan.rlfans.com] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090826213446/http://wigan.rlfans.com/readarticle.php?article_id=530 |date=26 August 2009 }} {{Great Britain 2006 Rugby League Tri Nations squad}} {{England 2008 Rugby League World Cup squad}} {{2012 Super League Dream Team}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hock, Gareth}} [[Category:1983 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Barrow Raiders players]] [[Category:Doping cases in rugby league]] [[Category:English rugby league players]] [[Category:England national rugby league team players]] [[Category:Featherstone Rovers players]] [[Category:Great Britain national rugby league team players]] [[Category:Leigh Leopards players]] [[Category:Rugby league second-rows]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Wigan]] [[Category:Salford RLFC players]] [[Category:Widnes Vikings players]] [[Category:Wigan Warriors players]]
